description: Top-level rights/ tree (D4): the join ledger. This is the ONLY link between a clinical KB identifier (an evidence.json source id, a rule id, a candidate id, a reference-ranges.json entry) and a rights/ record id (rights-records.json / rights-failures.json). No clinical JSON file (rules.json, candidates.json, evidence.json, reference-ranges.json) may carry an inline rights key of any kind, including under an `extensions.rights` path — that inline shape is explicitly rejected by D4 and is asserted against in tests/rights-substrate.test.mjs. EPR0-T1 creates this ledger empty; EPR0-T4 populates it while seeding the 6 triage-only rights records, joining each KB-cited source id to its rights_record_id. Entry shape: `clinical_identifier_type` names the kind of clinical identifier being joined (this task uses only `evidence_source_id`; a later phase may add other identifier types, e.g. a KB JSON file path, without changing this shape), `clinical_identifier` is the identifier value as it appears in the clinical KB JSON, and `rights_record_id` is the joined entry's id in rights/rights-records.json. EPR1-T1 (FR-WP1-01) adds a second entry against the existing `evidence_source_id`/`AAP2026_IDA` identifier, joining it to a new component-scoped record for modules/anemia/reference-ranges.json (the derived-fact channel) — a deliberate second join against the same identifier, not a duplicate; scripts/validate-rights.mjs's coverage gate treats every ledger entry's rights_record_id independently, so both the source-level and component-level records stay reachable. EPR1-T2 (FR-WP1-02/03) is expected to additionally introduce a dedicated `kb_json_file_path`-shaped identifier type for direct, file-path-level KB_JSON_FILES coverage; this entry does not substitute for that.

entries.notes: modules/anemia/reference-ranges.json (EPR1-T1, FR-WP1-01) — component-scoped record for the AAP2026_IDA Table 1 derived-fact channel (reference-ranges.json -> deriveFacts() -> all 91 rules). Second ledger entry against the same evidence_source_id as RR-AAP2026_IDA (source-level record) because reference-ranges.json's 32 values re-express the same AAP2026_IDA Table 1 that source-level record already covers; see rights-records.json RR-AAP2026_IDA-REFERENCE-RANGES.
